v0.1.0.description:
# NOTICE

This is a **beta release**. Anything & everything is subject to change *heavily* between versions, backwards compatibility is not guaranteed, and stability is not assured!

# Release notes

- Initial release. Have fun!

# Guide

This is a basic guide, which expects that you are already familiar with using custom worlds for Archipelago.

## Installing

1. Extract the `ringracers-ap.zip` into a new folder.
    - It is **highly recommended** that you create a new folder for Ring Racers AP, instead of installing on top of another Ring Racers install. I hold no responsibility for whatever might happen to your existing save files if you don't follow this advice; don't risk it!
2. Download `ringracers.apworld` and move it to your Archipelago's custom worlds folder.

## Connecting to a game

1. Open `ringracers-ap.exe`.
2. Configure your settings & profile from the menu before starting.
3. Set the Archipelago server's address & your slot's name in "Room Setup". Password is optional if the server didn't set one.
4. Select "Connect" to begin playing!
v0.1.0.title: Ring Racers AP v0.1.0

v0.1.1.description:
# NOTICE

This is a **beta release**. Anything & everything is subject to change *heavily* between versions, backwards compatibility is not guaranteed, and stability is not assured!

# Release notes

- Additional options have been added.
  - Added some individual toggles for different location types. (#60)
    - Challenges
    - Spray Cans
    - Prison Egg CD milestones
  - Enabled the `items` option for `accessibility`. (#52)
- Prison Egg CDs have been improved slightly. (#60)
  - Turn them off by default.
  - A hint is sent to the room when a CD is missed.
  - Fixed the CD count desyncing whenever the game was closed.
- Improved handling for skin availability communication with vanilla clients, to prevent issues with custom characters. (#57)
  - Prevents custom characters from potentially changing availability logic.
  - Fixes an assert in Debug builds.
- Fixed only one Spray Can in Sunbeam Paradise: Rings being tangible. (#63)
- Fixed a handful of follower challenges' logic referencing incorrect maps / conditions. (#61)
- Changed `starting_driver_pool` from a `NamedRange` to a `Choice`. (#60)

# Guide

This is a basic guide, which expects that you are already familiar with using custom worlds for Archipelago.

## Installing

1. Extract the `ringracers-ap_Windows.zip` into a new folder.
    - It is **highly recommended** that you create a new folder for Ring Racers AP, instead of installing on top of another Ring Racers install. I hold no responsibility for whatever might happen to your existing save files if you don't follow this advice; don't risk it!
    - There is no Linux build currently. I'd like to distribute these in the future, but for now, either compile yourself or use a Windows emulator. (I recommend [Bottles](https://usebottles.com/)!)
2. Download `ringracers.apworld` and move it to your Archipelago's custom worlds folder.

## Connecting to a game

1. Open `ringracers-ap.exe`.
2. Configure your settings & profile from the menu before starting.
3. Set the Archipelago server's address & your slot's name in "Room Setup". Password is optional if the server didn't set one.
4. Select "Connect" to begin playing!
v0.1.1.title: Ring Racers AP v0.1.1

v0.1.2.description:
# NOTICE

This is a **beta release**. Anything & everything is subject to change *heavily* between versions, backwards compatibility is not guaranteed, and stability is not assured!

# Guide

See here for a guide on how to install and use: [Dr. Robotnik's Ring Racers Randomizer Setup Guide](https://github.com/TehRealSalt/RingRacersAP/blob/v0.1.2/apworld/docs/setup_en.md)

# Release notes

- Added a proper Flatpak for Linux users! (#75) Some other improvements to the Linux build include...
    - Added a unique `.ringracers-ap` home folder, instead of reusing `.ringracers`.
    - Archipelago's data packages are now saved & loaded from the home folder. (#74)
- Added options for having Chao Keys as items. (#85)
    - Determine the number of Chao Keys in pool by setting the "Chao Key Ratio" option.
        - This is of a percentage of the number of locations in the world as-per your settings. For example, if you have 100 locations enabled, setting to 10% will attempt to create 10 Chao Keys.
        - It will try to replace KKD Medals to make room for these items first, before resorting to removing random filler items from the game.
    - Added option to alter the matches to play before KEYGEN occurs.
        - Set to 0 to turn off the ability to generate Chao Keys entirely.
- Difficulty mode handling has been improved:
    - Difficulties are now progressive items and are always granted in order. (#86)
    - Logic now takes into account that you need Vicious first in order to select Master. (#70)
        - This situation doesn't come up normally with difficulty being progressive now, but can still happen if manually given in starting items.
- Added location descriptions. (#90)
- Added option groups. (#90)
- Added a proper setup guide. (#96)
- Very long item names are now squished horizontally to fit. (#79)
- The room password is now masked using `***`. Stream safety! (#82, #83)
- Ring Racers AP's own version number is now displayed in more places. (#78)
- Many, *many* logic changes + fixes:
    - "Challenge - Driver: Eggrobo" properly expects having Knuckles first. (#72)
    - "Challenge - Driver: Ray" properly expects having Mighty first. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Follower: Mosqui" properly expects having Amy first. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Follower: Mag" properly expects having Mystic Melody first. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Driver: Gutbuster" properly expects having Time Attack. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Driver: Bark" properly expects having Time Attack, instead of Mystic Melody. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Driver: Battle Kukku XV" properly expects having Time Attack, instead of Mystic Melody. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Follower: UFO" properly expects having Prison Break, instead of Time Attack. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Follower: Canary" expects having any character with Cluckoid as a rival. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Sunbeam Paradise: Brakes", "Challenge - Sunbeam Paradise: Drifting", and "Challenge - Sunbeam Paradise: Items" now expect the correct Cup to be accessible. (#90)
    - "Challenge - Driver: Rouge" now requires having access to Prison Break. (#90)
        - Technically was always possible to do in Free Play, but it's very unintuitive to get there in single-player.
    - "Challenge - Follower: Chaclon" doesn't consider 200 medals in-logic any more, only expecting its usual condition. (#70)
        - This prevents having a potentially massive detour to do when receiving Time Attack.
    - Further nerfed "Challenge - Follower: Bowling Pin" to 10 seconds (from 60 seconds). (#95)
        - marui had 30 seconds at the end of a 14 cup run, so this should be extremely reasonable even when you have to intentionally go for it.
- Fixed CD hints being given when a CD was actually collected. (#94)
- Fixed the item notification's format being incorrect. (#69)
- Fixed the "Archipelago" header from Room Setup infecting other Options menus. (#74)
- Fixed a minor typo on the in-game item label for "Alt. Music: Mega Collision Chaos". (#88)
- Renamed "Driver: Chaos" to "Driver: Chaos Zero", to better match how they're usually referred by the game. (#90)
- Fixed release automation for Windows builds not distributing Dr. Mingw's .dll files. (#81)
- Fixed Windows builds displaying "UNCOMMITTED CHANGES" on the title screen. (#78)

**The full diff is here**: https://github.com/TehRealSalt/RingRacersAP/compare/v0.1.1...v0.1.2
v0.1.2.title: Ring Racers AP v0.1.2
